spec_match.rationale: Leverages native PerformanceObservers for LCP reset values nicely, but pollutes the raw log stream on supported browsers with duplicate simulated soft-navigation rows.

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive route selectors that immediately paint LCP candidates, with visual timeline segment graphs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligned with LCP per Route description.

edge_cases.rationale: Properly gates simulated LCP updates when API is supported, but fails to isolate simulated soft-nav logs.

references.rationale: Violates repository hard rule: does not contain a link to chromestatus.com/feature/5144837209194496.

design_system.rationale: Visual diagram block is outstanding, matching style requirements.

openQuestions.title: Isolate simulated soft-navigation logs on supported browsers
openQuestions.detail: The raw entry log receives a duplicate soft-nav entry on supported browsers because navigate() unconditionally appends a simulated event, while navObs also appends the real event. The inline log should be wrapped in if (!softNavSupported || !lcpSupported).

openQuestions.title: Add ChromeStatus link (hard rule violation)
openQuestions.detail: The page does not contain a link to chromestatus.com/feature/5144837209194496, which violates the repository hard rule that every concept page must include a ChromeStatus link.
